message.content:
To verify your OpenClaw system is set up correctly, run through these quick checks:

1) Basic power + hardware sanity

System powers on without resets or overheating

Motors, servos, and sensors respond (no jittering or dead channels)

Wiring matches your schematic (especially motor drivers + limit switches)

2) Controller / firmware check

Connect to your controller (e.g., Arduino, Raspberry Pi, etc.) via USB

Upload or run a simple test program:

Move one motor at a time

Trigger each sensor individually

Confirm:

No compile/upload errors

Serial output (if used) shows expected values

3) Input / control test

If using a controller (joystick, buttons, app):

Each input maps correctly (no reversed axes unless intended)

Dead zones and sensitivity feel right

If autonomous:

Run a dry cycle and watch decision logic step-by-step

4) Movement validation

Run the claw through full range:

Open/close claw smoothly

Lift/lower arm without stalling

Check for:

Grinding noises

Uneven motion

Missed steps (stepper systems)

5) Safety checks

Emergency stop (if you built one) works instantly

Limit switches stop motion at boundaries

No overheating after a few minutes of operation

6) Repeatability test (important)

Run the same pickup cycle 5–10 times:

Does it land in the same position each time?

Does grip strength stay consistent?

7) Debug signals (if available)

Use serial logs or LEDs to confirm:

State transitions (idle → move → grab → lift)

Sensor triggers happen at the right time

Quick “it’s working” checklist

You’re in good shape if:

All axes move correctly

Inputs behave predictably

It completes a full grab cycle without failure

No overheating, resets, or random behavior

If something feels off

Tell me:

Your controller (Arduino? Pi? custom board?)

Motor type (servo, stepper, DC)

What exactly happens vs. expected

I can help you pinpoint the issue fast.
